title: When is Eva 30s US Theatrical Release
display_title: When is Eva 30s US Theatrical Release
wikitext:
[]: When is Eva 3.0's US Theatrical Release?             Quote Post by ApolloIVtv » Fri Oct 18, 2013 12:15 am Hey, is there any confirmation or speculation on a US 3.33 theatrical release yet? I know Funimation did it with the last 2 movies, but I heard they showed 2.22 in less theaters in its release, so I'm just sort of speculating on whether or not they'll do they same for 3.33, or just not release it at all and go straight to DVD/BD.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Fri Oct 18, 2013 12:21 am When Funimation acquired the first 2 films (1.0 and 2.0), the process was convention screenings, then theatrical screenings (dub or sub depended on quickly they finished the dub), then the Bluray/DVD release. Since the 3.33 DVD/Bluray is rumored to get a U.S. release of Feburary 18th 2014, sounds like the next step is theatrical screenings. I'd assume info about when and where will probably be officially released this or next month (November).

[]:             Quote Post by Noah » Mon Oct 21, 2013 4:59 am I'm still waiting for 1.11 on iTunes.

[]:             Quote Post by hui43210 » Mon Oct 21, 2013 10:32 am Noah wrote: I'm still waiting for 1.11 on iTunes.

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Mon Oct 21, 2013 11:32 am Aw, man! I was hoping for some actual release information. Noah wrote: I'm still waiting for 1.11 on iTunes.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Mon Oct 21, 2013 12:01 pm FreakyFilmFan4ever wrote: Aw, man! I was hoping for some actual release information.

[]:             Quote Post by Gus Hanson » Mon Oct 21, 2013 12:06 pm If that February 18, 2014 date for the Blu Ray/DVD release turns out to be true, that would fall on my birthday so that would be a sweet present.

[]:             Quote Post by pwhodges » Mon Oct 21, 2013 1:49 pm Heh! Also my wife's birthday (a big one) - but she has absolutely no interest in anime; I'm not sure she even knows what it is.

[]:             Quote Post by Warren Peace » Mon Oct 21, 2013 3:04 pm I'm hardly an expert on the intricacies of the anime market, but why not aim for a Holiday release? They're screening the dub, the disc has to be almost ready to go, right? I don't see why February is more advantageous.

[]:             Quote Post by pwhodges » Mon Oct 21, 2013 5:37 pm Less competition from blockbusters?

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Mon Oct 21, 2013 6:07 pm I say "fuck it", go out, buy the DVD/Blu-ray, and do what I did, get a bunch of awesome people together and watch it as a group. I'm fairly sure the experience would be better than seeing it in a theater. In my opinion, these theatrical runs are an unnecessary hold-up. If they were doing it to qualify for the Academy Awards, that would be one thing, but I know 1.0 was ineligible, and I can't find a source for 2.0 qualifying, just that I know it never made it into the nominees.

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Mon Oct 21, 2013 7:40 pm While theatrical releases do give me the opportunity to experience the same movie on a big screen akin to it's initial Japanese release (as well as a possibility of seeing the X.0 version as opposed to the X.XX version), the trend in media is drifting away from the theaters anyway. A lot of movies are playing out much better on DVD, Blu-ray, or simply through Video On Demand and the Internet than they ever did in theaters. Media is becoming more in the control of the common man than it is the rich man or the corporations, and an extravagant theatrical release shouldn't be necessary to a film's success anymore. (It's starting to produce more box-office bombs than usual.)

[]:             Quote Post by ApolloIVtv » Wed Oct 23, 2013 1:25 am I just want to see a full-fledged, Japanese, animated feature (that's not Ghibli) on the big screen, at least once. And I would like for that to be Eva. And I was always one to prefer theater over home on the television, but only for maybe the first couple viewings. It's especially great when everyone in the theater is a big fan of the material, like whenever you go to a midnight show for a popular movie that has a huge fanbase. When I went to go see Pacific Rim at midnight, everyone was cheering, laughing, and having a good time, and now it's on blu-ray and they can watch it with their friends. I'm assuming that people going to see 3.0 at a theater are eva fans, seeing that no casual viewer would go see it, or even a casual eva fan. And from the description of the NYCC screening, it seemed like they had a lot of fun. That is to say though that a lot of people may not go, or the theater I'll go to may be some low-end one with a screen smaller than my tv. (Still going though)

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Wed Oct 23, 2013 11:42 am I guess being one who has been to a lot of conventions, I'm sort of spoiled in that regard. I've watched 1.0 and 2.0 with audiences, and it was no big deal. The only time I watched an Eva film with an audience and had a great time was seeing D&R and EoE at Katsucon in 2002. But a big part of that was that there was very limited access to it at the time, and it was my first full viewing of it. Some people may enjoy seeing these movies in commercial theaters, but given that they're only shown in dub is a turn-off to some fans, myself included, and it just seems like a novelty that delays everyone else from getting a legitimate copy. I would be curious to see accurate sales figures, with some sort of bottom-line amount of tickets sold. Box Office Mojo's numbers aren't terribly encouraging. http://boxofficemojo.com/search/?q=evangelion

[]:             Quote Post by Warren Peace » Wed Oct 23, 2013 12:38 pm The theatrical run is (AFAIK) a condition set by Khara. I wonder what the thinking is behind that. Like Eva Monkey said, if they could get an Academy nomination out of it that would be one thing, but the Oscars are prejudiced against non-Miyazaki anime anyway. I don't see the upside in asking your U.S. distributor to burn money before selling in an already tough market.

[]:             Quote Post by hui43210 » Wed Oct 23, 2013 1:05 pm ApolloIVtv wrote: I just want to see a full-fledged, Japanese, animated feature (that's not Ghibli) on the big screen, at least once. And I would like for that to be Eva. And I was always one to prefer theater over home on the television, but only for maybe the first couple viewings. It's especially great when everyone in the theater is a big fan of the material, like whenever you go to a midnight show for a popular movie that has a huge fanbase. When I went to go see Pacific Rim at midnight, everyone was cheering, laughing, and having a good time, and now it's on blu-ray and they can watch it with their friends. I'm assuming that people going to see 3.0 at a theater are eva fans, seeing that no casual viewer would go see it, or even a casual eva fan. And from the description of the NYCC screening, it seemed like they had a lot of fun. That is to say though that a lot of people may not go, or the theater I'll go to may be some low-end one with a screen smaller than my tv. (Still going though)
